summary: ChatGPT owns a single canonical packet.definition artifact. Local realization is governed by scm.pattern. Approval and realization binding are mandatory, standalone, and basis-fingerprint-bound. The contract is intentionally shallow, temporary, and removable.

transitions: Any semantic change to the approved basis forces DEFINITION_APPROVED -> REQUIRES_REAPPROVAL
REQUIRES_REAPPROVAL -> DEFINITION_UNDER_REVIEW only after a revised definition is resubmitted
boundary_rules: ChatGPT may only create or revise packet.definition
Human approval is required before realization
A valid scm.pattern binding is required before approval and realization
scm.pattern governs local realization after approval
unchanged required inputs must not trigger regeneration

precedence: delegated validation schemas declared by the authority manifest
conflict_resolution: workflow docs define lifecycle and review semantics
schemas define object shape and field validity

required_field_notes.sidecar_status: Must declare that the implementation is temporary and shallow.
required_field_notes.approval_basis_inputs: Must enumerate the approval basis inputs that are fingerprinted for approval validity.
required_field_notes.delegated_surfaces: Must name delegated surfaces and their subordinate roles.
required_field_notes.deprecation_note: Must state that deeper normalization is deferred and removal should remain mechanical.

sidecar_notes: This is a temporary sidecar implementation.
Keep the contract shallow and easy to remove later.
Do not embed this deeper into kernel-core than necessary.

artifacts.realization_rule: No packet may be approved, realized, or closed without a valid scm.pattern binding.
artifacts.binding_rule: scm.pattern.binding.json is mandatory and part of the approval and realization boundary.

basis: approval-relevant authority refs named in the authority manifest
approval.fingerprint_rule: Approval is valid only for the exact canonicalized approval basis.
approval.approval_basis_fingerprint_rule: The approval basis fingerprint is computed from canonicalized packet.definition.json, scm.pattern.binding.json, control/scm.pattern/authority.manifest.json, and the approval-relevant authority refs declared in the authority manifest.

state_rule.on_semantic_change_after_approval: Mark the prior packet.approval.json verdict STALE and set packet.definition.workflow_state to REQUIRES_REAPPROVAL and packet.definition.review_state to REQUIRES_REAPPROVAL.
state_rule.resubmission_transition: A revised definition must move from REQUIRES_REAPPROVAL to DEFINITION_UNDER_REVIEW before a fresh approval can be issued.
state_rule.realization_gate: REALIZED_VIA_SCM_PATTERN is allowed only when packet.definition.workflow_state is DEFINITION_APPROVED, packet.definition.review_state is DEFINITION_APPROVED, and packet.approval.json carries a matching approval_basis_fingerprint.
state_rule.authority_manifest_change_rule: Any semantic change to control/scm.pattern/authority.manifest.json marks prior approval STALE.

approval.non_semantic_rule: Formatting-only changes do not invalidate approval.
approval.supersession_rule: A fresh packet.approval.json supersedes any older approval artifacts for the same packet_id.

state_mapping_rules: If workflow_state = REQUESTED, review_state may be absent.
If workflow_state in {REALIZED_VIA_SCM_PATTERN, CLOSED}, review_state = DEFINITION_APPROVED.

required_sections.scm_pattern: Summary plus binding reference to the standalone realization contract.
required_sections.review_state: Review-only substate aligned to the workflow lifecycle.

scm_pattern_binding.purpose: Explicit local realization binding for the adopted scm.pattern route.

human_synthesis.source: Derived from structured machine content only.
requirements: Must be sufficient for human reconstruction of packet intent without source code
Must distinguish authoritative fields from explanatory prose

regen.rule: Regeneration is blocked unless required inputs change.
regen.fingerprinting: Use canonicalized JSON with stable key order and no whitespace sensitivity.

migration_notes: Treat any existing kernel compatibility paths as temporary delegated validation only, not a second authority root.
Keep the sidecar shallow so removal is mechanical later.
Do not introduce separate plan or implement artifact families in the final model.
